target.title: fix(slack): fail unconfirmed threaded deliveries
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Slack threaded payload delivery could still be reported as successful when Slack did not actually confirm the requested origin thread. The previous guard checked `result.threadTs`, but `sendMessageSlack` may preserve the requested `opts.threadTs` as a compatibility fallback in the public result and receipt. That meant an unconfirmed threaded send could look confirmed to the outbound adapter.

This PR is related to #96692, but it is scoped to the fail-closed confirmation contract for threaded Slack payload sends.

## Why This Change Was Made
The adapter needs a value that only comes from Slack's API response, not from OpenClaw's requested send options. This patch adds `confirmedThreadTs` to `SlackSendResult` only when Slack returns `message.thread_ts`, keeps the existing `threadTs`/receipt behavior for compatibility, and makes the outbound adapter validate threaded payload delivery against `confirmedThreadTs`.

That separates "requested or canonical thread id for callers" from "Slack confirmed this specific thread in the response", which is the distinction needed for the fail-closed guard.

## User Impact
Threaded Slack block/payload sends now fail clearly when Slack returns a message without confirming the requested thread, or when Slack confirms a different thread. Confirmed threaded sends still succeed and include the normal Slack result fields.

This avoids silently treating generated-but-not-confirmed threaded completions as delivered in the expected Slack thread.

## Evidence
Head commit: `a5877d86b5d3c249f0b8e1ac6e929139b53b0d4f`.

Local targeted behavior proof:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/slack/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts extensions/slack/src/send.blocks.test.ts` -> 2 files passed, 36 tests passed.
- `pnpm oxfmt --check extensions/slack/src/send.ts extensions/slack/src/outbound-adapter.ts extensions/slack/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts extensions/slack/src/send.blocks.test.ts` -> all 4 touched files formatted.
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` -> passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions` -> passed.
- `git diff --check` -> no whitespace errors.

Behavior cases covered by the tests:

- `sendMessageSlack` leaves `confirmedThreadTs` unset when Slack only returns a message id and OpenClaw only has the requested `threadTs` fallback.
- `sendMessageSlack` sets `confirmedThreadTs` when Slack returns canonical `message.thread_ts` for text, chunked, and block sends.
- `slackOutbound.sendPayload` rejects threaded payload delivery when the result only echoes the requested `threadTs` without `confirmedThreadTs`.
- `slackOutbound.sendPayload` rejects a different confirmed Slack thread and accepts the matching confirmed thread.
target.author: lin-hongkuan
